questions.prompt: You will receive $2,000 in 7 years. The discount rate is 5%. What is the present value? Answer in dollars to the nearest cent.
You invest $10,000 today at 8% annual interest. What is its future value in 10 years? Answer in dollars to the nearest cent.
Using the Rule of 72, approximately how long does an investment earning 9% per year take to double?
questions.explanation: Rule of 72: doubling time ≈ 72 / r = 72 / 9 = 8 years. (Exact answer is ~8.04 years.)

questions.prompt: Which of the three rules of time travel forbids you to add $100 today to $100 in five years without first discounting or compounding?
choices: Rule 1: only same-date cash flows can be combined
Rule 2: compound to move forward in time
Rule 3: discount to move backward in time
questions.explanation: Rule 1 establishes that cash flows at different dates are different goods and cannot be combined directly.

furtherReading.title: Mortgage rates were supposed to come down. Instead, they're rising. Here's why
furtherReading.why: Concrete TVM story: 30-year mortgage payments more than doubled since 2020 because of compounding higher interest rates, directly demonstrating present-value sensitivity to discount rates.
